summary.intent: Address Codex automated review feedback on PR #683 in the ars-ui repo by fixing accessibility and behavioral bugs in editable input components, then shepherd the PR through a second Codex review pass and CI.
summary.outcome: All six first-pass Codex threads were replied to and resolved via commit e9f5f2e2. A second Codex pass raised two more issues (root id attribute and UTF-16 maxlength semantics), both were fixed, tests updated, and committed as 'Address Editable root and maxlength review feedback'. All CI checks were green except late-stage matrix jobs still running at session end.

repo: The repo uses a Codex bot (@codex) for automated PR review; triggering a new pass is done via 'gh pr comment <PR> --body "@codex review"'.
Codex review state is tracked via GitHub reactions (eyes = in progress) and GraphQL reviewThreads API.
The label 'snapshot-reviewed' is applied to the PR after snapshot changes are accepted; it must be re-applied after each push.
Snapshot tests use 'cargo insta' and must be accepted with 'cargo insta accept' after intentional changes; '--unreferenced=reject' is used to catch stale snapshots.
The project has a 'cargo xtask spec validate' command for spec conformance checking.
Native HTML maxlength semantics use UTF-16 code units, not Unicode scalar values; the internal clamp must match this to avoid splitting surrogate pairs.
Coverage is measured with 'cargo llvm-cov' targeting specific modules.

code.finding: Max-length clamping logic was changed to count UTF-16 code units (matching native maxlength) rather than Unicode scalar values, to avoid splitting multibyte characters.
Root element was missing an id attribute; added to match spec and allow external label association.
Spec text and focused tests for maxlength semantics were updated alongside the implementation change.

workflow: Codex thread resolution requires two separate API calls: a comment reply (REST) and a resolve mutation (GraphQL).
CI jobs are run in a matrix; later jobs (coverage, feature-flag variants, release verification) run after core jobs (format, clippy, unit tests, snapshots) complete.
The session explicitly avoided running mutation testing per prior user instruction; only focused unit, snapshot, spec, format, and clippy checks were run.
Polling CI and Codex state was done with 120-second sleep loops combining reactions, GraphQL thread count, and 'gh pr checks'.

friction: The snapshot accept step required two attempts ('cargo insta accept -p ars-components' then 'cargo insta accept') before succeeding, suggesting a scoping issue with the -p flag.
Codex review took multiple 2-minute polling cycles (~10+ minutes) to complete the second pass, creating idle wait time.
The 'snapshot-reviewed' label had to be manually re-applied after each push, which is an easy step to forget in the workflow.
open_items: Late-stage CI matrix jobs (coverage, Dioxus/Leptos/subsystem feature flags, release verification) were still pending at session end and need to pass before merge.
Mutation testing was explicitly skipped per prior instruction; a full mutation run may still be needed before merge.
The second Codex pass produced only two threads which were addressed, but a third Codex pass was not triggered — whether another review cycle is needed is unresolved.
